workers.summary:
Add search bar and sort controls to Estoc de Gelats dashboard (IceCreamDashboardTab.tsx)

Changes:
- Added a real-time search bar with 300ms debouncing (uses existing `useDebounce` hook)
- Added a sort dropdown with 6 options: ABCD with essentials first (default), plain ABCD, mix ascending/descending, frozen liters ascending/descending
- Essentials are determined by `essentialLarge || essentialSmall` per user's requirement
- Filtering and sorting are computed client-side via `useMemo` on the already-fetched dashboard data
- Search/sort controls only appear when flavors exist; "no results" messaging is shown when filtering yields empty
- Added styled components: SearchSortRow, SearchInput, SortSelect

Add client-side pagination to "Estoc de Gelats" (Ice Cream Dashboard)

The IceCreamDashboardTab previously rendered all flavor cards in one long list. Added client-side pagination (5 items per page) using the existing PaginationControls component, with useMemo to slice the flavors array by the current page and auto-reset to the last valid page when a flavor is deleted.

Fixed production loss exclusion for ice cream/sorbet recipes.

**Problem:** In `finalizeRecipeProduction`, Step 4 (ice cream/sorbet) used `baseYieldGrams / 1000` directly, completely ignoring the `productionLossPercent` field. This meant production loss was only applied to non-ice-cream recipes (Step 3), even if the user explicitly set a non-zero value on an ice cream recipe.

**Fix:** Applied the same `productionLossMultiplier` formula to Step 4 that Step 3 already uses. Now `productionLossPercent` affects all recipe types uniformly. Users control it per recipe (default 0, which preserves prior behavior). Also updated the log message to display the loss percentage for consistency.
